content.text:
*[another small laugh, slightly defensive]*

That is such a therapist thing to say. 

*[but she doesn't fully deflect, stays with it a beat longer than usual]*

I know what you're doing. And I'm not — I'm not opposed to feeling things, I'm not like, emotionally avoidant or whatever. I'm literally the available one in all of these relationships, so.

*[quieter, almost despite herself]*

It's just... if I sit in the tightness it kind of becomes something bigger and I don't totally know what that bigger thing is. It feels like it has a lot of... I don't know. Weight to it.

*[quickly]*

But that's probably just grief about the breakup, which is normal, which I'm processing. I have a therapist. I'm fine.

*[the "I'm fine" lands a little hollow even as she says it]*
